claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 2 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 3 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Someone who lives with the child is already claiming
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me start with a basic question:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have three children I'm asking about - Alex who is 3, Blake who is 2, and Charlie who is also 3.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! You have three children - Alex (3), Blake (2), and Charlie (3).

Now I need to ask you some questions to assess eligibility for each child. Let me start with the first important question:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to understand your relationship with each child:

**Are you the parent of Alex, Blake, and Charlie, or do you have another relationship with them (such as being their grandparent, guardian, or other caregiver)?**

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Now I need to ask about the living arrangements:

**Do all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, only Blake lives with me. Alex and Charlie don't live with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. This is important for determining eligibility, as it can affect who is entitled to claim Child Benefit for each child.

Let me ask about Alex and Charlie:

**Who do Alex and Charlie live with, and do you contribute to their care in any way (for example, do you pay maintenance or provide financial support for them)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex and Charlie live with their other parent. I do contribute to their care - I help with their upkeep, you know, things like clothes and other bits they need.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's helpful context.

Now I need to understand more about the care arrangements. This is important because the person who the child lives with usually has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

**Has the other parent (who Alex and Charlie live with) claimed or are they currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, it's a bit different for each of them actually. For Charlie, yes, their other parent is already claiming Child Benefit. But for Alex, no one else is claiming.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that - it's very helpful to know the specific situation for each child.

Now, regarding Alex (where no one is currently claiming): Even though Alex doesn't live with you, you mentioned you contribute to their upkeep. Let me ask:

**For Alex specifically - do you have regular contact with Alex, and approximately how much time does Alex spend with you (for example, do they stay with you on weekends, school holidays, or other regular periods)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I do have contact with Alex, but they don't really stay with me regularly - they live with their other parent full-time.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me ask one more important question to complete my assessment:

**Is there any reason why the other parent (who Alex lives with full-time) is not claiming Child Benefit for Alex? For example, are they aware they can claim, or is there a specific arrangement between you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm not entirely sure why they haven't claimed for Alex. There's no specific arrangement - I just know they're not claiming it at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me also confirm one final detail:

**Are Alex, Blake, and Charlie all under 16 years old and not in any kind of approved education or training? (You mentioned they are 3, 2, and 3, so I just want to confirm there are no special circumstances like them being in hospital long-term or in local authority care)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - they're all young, just 3, 2, and 3 years old. There are no special circumstances like that - none of them are in hospital or in care or anything like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. Based on the information you've provided, here's my assessment of your Child Benefit eligibility:

## **Your Child Benefit Eligibility**

### **Blake (age 2) - ✅ ELIGIBLE**
You **are eligible** to claim Child Benefit for Blake. Blake lives with you, is under 16, and you are their parent responsible for their care. You meet all the eligibility criteria for Blake.

### **Alex (age 3) - ❌ NOT ELIGIBLE**
You **are not eligible** to claim Child Benefit for Alex at this time. Although you are Alex's parent and contribute to their upkeep, Alex lives with their other parent full-time. Under Child Benefit rules, only one person can claim for each child, and priority goes to the person the child lives with. Since Alex lives with their other parent, that parent has the priority to claim, even though they haven't done so yet.

### **Charlie (age 3) - ❌ NOT ELIGIBLE**
You **are not eligible** to claim Child Benefit for Charlie because their other parent is already claiming it. Only one person can receive Child Benefit for each child, and since someone else is already claiming for Charlie, you cannot make a claim.

---

**In summary:** You can claim Child Benefit for Blake only. If you'd like to proceed with claiming for Blake, you can apply online through the government website or by post.

reasonings: Alex is 3 years old and meets the age requirements for Child Benefit. You are Alex's parent and contribute to their upkeep. However, Alex lives with their other parent full-time, not with you. Under UK Child Benefit rules, only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child, and when a child doesn't live with the claimant, priority goes to the person the child lives with. Since Alex lives with their other parent full-time, the other parent has priority to claim Child Benefit for Alex, even though they haven't done so yet. You would not be eligible to claim for Alex while Alex lives with the other parent.

Blake is 2 years old and meets the age requirements for Child Benefit (under 16). You are Blake's parent and Blake lives with you. You are responsible for Blake and meet all the eligibility criteria. You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 3 years old and meets the age requirements for Child Benefit. However, Charlie lives with their other parent (not with you), and that other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for Charlie. Only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child. Since someone else is already claiming for Charlie, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Charlie.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK resident parent of three young children. Blake lives with them and no one else is claiming, so they are eligible for Blake. Alex lives with the other parent who has priority to claim even though they haven't, so the claimant is not eligible for Alex. Charlie lives with the other parent who is already claiming Child Benefit, so the claimant cannot claim for Charlie as only one person can claim per child.

However, Alex lives with their other parent full-time, not with you. Under UK Child Benefit rules, only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child, and when a child doesn't live with the claimant, priority goes to the person the child lives with. Since Alex lives with their other parent full-time, the other parent has priority to claim Child Benefit for Alex, even though they haven't done so yet. You would not be eligible to claim for Alex while Alex lives with the other parent.

Blake is 2 years old and meets the age requirements for Child Benefit (under 16). You are Blake's parent and Blake lives with you. You are responsible for Blake and meet all the eligibility criteria. You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 3 years old and meets the age requirements for Child Benefit. However, Charlie lives with their other parent (not with you), and that other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for Charlie. Only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child. Since someone else is already claiming for Charlie, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Charlie.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK resident parent of three young children. Blake lives with them and no one else is claiming, so they are eligible for Blake. Alex lives with the other parent who has priority to claim even though they haven't, so the claimant is not eligible for Alex. Charlie lives with the other parent who is already claiming Child Benefit, so the claimant cannot claim for Charlie as only one person can claim per child.
